channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Texas Republican Candidates Face Off in Key Senate Race
item.description: Voters in Texas head to the polls on Tuesday for a Republican Senate runoff between the incumbent, John Cornyn, and a Trump-backed challenger, Ken Paxton.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon

media:content.media:credit: Daniel Cole/Reuters; Jordan Vonderhaar for The New York Times

item.title: Evolving Warfare Connects the Conflicts in Ukraine and Iran
item.description: Aspects such as drone technology and diplomacy show how the wars intersect on the battlefield and in global alignments, providing a model for future conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Assessing the damaged buildings following Russian strikes in Ukraine’s capital, Kyiv, on Sunday.

item.title: Global Oil Price Rises After U.S. Strikes in Iran Cloud Peace Deal
item.description: Optimism for a resolution to the conflict and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz was checked after the United States said it had carried out strikes on missile launch sites in Iran.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Vehículos eléctricos de 140.000 dólares y oro: el auge del mercado de lujo nacional chino
item.description: A medida que la economía china se ralentiza, los consumidores se decantan cada vez más por los productos de lujo nacionales, desafiando el dominio de las marcas europeas establecidas desde hace tiempo.
item.dc:creator: Joy Dong

media:content.media:credit: Gilles Sabrié para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Li Maozai en su sedan Maextro S800, fabricado por Huawei y JAC Motors.

item.title: New York Leads in Controlling Heat-Trapping Methane. That Could Change.
item.description: An overhaul of the state’s 2019 climate law proposed by Gov. Kathy Hochul would alter how the potent greenhouse gas is measured.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Howard

media:content.media:description: Gov. Kathy Hochul has said that a potential shift in how New York measures methane would align it with most other states.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 26, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘inchoate’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 31 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Por qué no puedo dejar a mi esposo infiel serial
item.description: Nuestra columnista de Pregúntale a la terapeuta, Lori Gottlieb, aconseja a una lectora que se siente atrapada a pesar de una serie de infidelidades.
item.dc:creator: Lori Gottlieb

item.title: Word of the Day: inchoate
item.description: This word has appeared in 31 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: La autocomplacencia de Trump ahonda los temores su partido en las elecciones intermedias
item.description: “Las estupideces están acabando con nuestras posibilidades”, dijo un senador republicano.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher and Patricia Mazzei
item.category: White House East Wing and Ballroom (Washington, DC)

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente Donald Trump

item.title: Iceland, Rattled by Trump’s Greenland Threats, Weighs Joining the E.U.
item.description: Iceland has stood apart from the rest of Europe. But President Trump’s threats to Greenland have provoked a reconsideration.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Sergey Ponomarev

media:content.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times

item.title: Inside India’s Manipur State, Where Violence and Division Are Routine
item.description: Three years after riots tore apart Manipur, the state remains in disarray. Barbed wire and armed checkpoints made it difficult for Times reporters to cross, even before the recent clashes.
item.dc:creator: Hari Kumar and Atul Loke

media:content.media:description: Security personnel at a buffer-zone checkpoint between Imphal and Churachandpur in Manipur, India, in March.

item.title: Corrections: May 26, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Tuesday, May 26, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: In New World, Gore Rethinks How to Save It
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, May 26, 2026.

item.title: Sonny Rollins: 12 Essential Albums
item.description: The towering saxophonist, who died at 95, was a master of living in the moment. Listen to some of his most compelling work, onstage and in the studio.
item.dc:creator: Hank Shteamer

media:content.media:description: Sonny Rollins performing in Nice, France, in 2009.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 26, 2026
item.description: Brad Lively shows us the anatomy of a good crossword puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: Sonny Rollins, Giant of the Jazz Saxophone, Is Dead at 95
item.description: Even by the standards of a music that prizes individuality, he stood out, as both a musician and a personality.
item.dc:creator: Peter Keepnews
item.category: Schomburg Center for Research in Black Culture

media:content.media:credit: Stephanie Berger for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Sonny Rollins in 2006. He flirted with the avant-garde, jazz-rock fusion and other styles over the years, but he was ultimately unclassifiable.

item.title: U.S. Carries Out Renewed Strikes in Southern Iran
item.description: Military officials said that the strikes targeted missile sites near a major Iranian port that threatened U.S. ships.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Ships near Bandar Abbas, Iran, in May.

item.title: Map: 6.9-Magnitude Earthquake Shakes Chile
item.description: View the location of the quake’s epicenter and shake area.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee and Bea Malsky

item.title: Reglas para envejecer bien
item.description: Mantenerse vivo en la vejez es un arte que por lo general requiere evitar los pasos en falso.
item.dc:creator: Roger Rosenblatt

item.title: En la ciudad en el centro de la crisis del ébola, ‘el virus nos lleva mucha ventaja’
item.description: El virus mortal se ha propagado de forma alarmante en la República Democrática del Congo durante meses. La respuesta apenas está tomando forma.
item.dc:creator: Declan Walsh and Arlette Bashizi

media:content.media:description: Desinfección del mercado público el sábado en Bunia, en el noreste de la República Democrática del Congo, como parte de la respuesta a la reaparición del virus del Ébola.

item.title: Netanyahu Says Israel Plans to Intensify Attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon
item.description: After the prime minister made the announcement, the Israeli military said it had struck more than 70 Hezbollah sites in the past day.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Smoke rising in the city of Tyre, Lebanon, on Monday.

item.title: Ride-Share Drivers in Massachusetts Formally Unionize
item.description: The App Drivers Union said it was the first organization in the country to be formally certified to represent drivers for apps such as Uber and Lyft.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Holpuch

media:content.media:credit: David L. Ryan/The Boston Globe, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: An app drivers union in Massachusetts was certified after a ballot measure in 2024 gave the drivers the right to collectively bargain.

item.title: Are We Pandemic Ready?
item.description: Hantavirus and Ebola are reminders that outbreaks are inevitable, and that the world must work together to contain them and prevent the next pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

media:content.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times

item.title: Main Takeaways From Pope Leo’s Encyclical on A.I.
item.description: In his letter, the first American pontiff called for putting care for humans at the heart of technological change.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Graham and Elizabeth Dias

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV speaks during the presentation of “Magnifica Humanitas,” his first encyclical, at the Vatican on Monday.

item.title: Guerra en Irán: Trump pospone los temas más difíciles para reabrir el estrecho de Ormuz
item.description: El presidente Trump aclama el acuerdo con Irán como innovador, aunque admite que no se ha negociado del todo. El arsenal nuclear, el enriquecimiento y los misiles no se han discutido.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

media:content.media:description: El estrecho de Ormuz ha sido un punto central desde el inicio del conflicto.

item.title: After Defeat, Massie Opens Door to a 2028 Run. Which Office Is Unclear.
item.description: Representative Thomas Massie of Kentucky, a critic of President Trump who lost his primary last week, filed papers for another candidacy but said he was not sure which office he might seek.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kruse

media:content.media:description: Representative Thomas Massie of Kentucky, who lost his primary last week, filed paperwork to run for office in 2028, though he said that he was keeping his options open.

item.title: Ferrari Unveils Luce, Its First Fully Electric Car
item.description: The Italian sports car maker unveiled Luce, its first all-electric model, on Monday amid wider worries about the luxury E.V. market.
item.dc:creator: Bernhard Warner

item.title: ¿Qué dice la primera encíclica del papa León XIV?
item.description: El documento marca una poderosa incursión del líder de la Iglesia católica romana en el debate sobre el uso indebido o excesivo de la inteligencia artificial.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich, Elisabetta Povoledo and Elizabeth Dias

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV at the presentation of his first encyclical letter, “Magnifica Humanitas,” at the Vatican on Monday. In it, he outlined his desire to protect human dignity and agency in an age of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Gov. Sherrill Demands Access to ICE Facility as Hunger Strike Widens
item.description: After Gov. Mikie Sherrill joined protesters at an immigration jail in New Jersey, the standoff grew tense, with ICE agents deploying pepper balls and spray.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu and Nate Schweber

media:content.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Mikie Sherrill, left, joined protesters and other elected officials at Delaney Hall as a hunger strike by detainees entered its fourth day.

item.title: ‘A Dangerous Bluff’: Carney Likens Alberta Referendum to Brexit
item.description: The Canadian prime minister, who was the governor of the Bank of England when Britain voted to leave the European Union, said the Alberta referendum on separating from Canada could backfire.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff

media:content.media:credit: Ethan Cairns/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada said of Brexit, “They’re still, 10 years later, trying to undo what people didn’t think they were voting for.”

item.title: ‘Bing Bong!’ The Knicks’ Watch Party Outside the Garden Is Dead.
item.description: The police say that celebrations outside of Madison Square Garden have gotten out of control. Fans and insiders say the chaos has been driven by social media influencers.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich, Chelsia Rose Marcius and Debra Kamin

media:content.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Fans have been celebrating outside Madison Square Garden as the Knicks appear poised to reach the N.B.A. finals. But the celebrations have grown rowdy, and the city has decided to cancel the crowd-watching parties.

item.title: Trump Urges More Countries to Normalize Relations With Israel as Part of Iran Deal
item.description: President Trump called for countries in the Middle East to join the Abraham Accords as part of a peace deal with Iran. Analysts say it is unlikely.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump during a Memorial Day ceremony at Arlington National Cemetery on Monday.

item.title: Parts of Britain Hit With First Heat Wave of the Year
item.description: Large areas of Britain were hit with their first heat wave of the year on Monday. Temperatures in Greater London’s suburb of Teddington matched a 1944 record for the hottest day in May.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:description: Beachgoers in Brighton, England, on Monday. The country is facing its first heat wave of the season.

item.title: Lizzo Addresses the Backlash and Performs Live at The New York Times
item.description: During a special edition of “Popcast,” the singer discussed her new album and the public fallout from a series of lawsuits. Then she performed a stripped-down set.
item.dc:creator: Joe Coscarelli and Jon Caramanica

media:content.media:credit: Thea Traff for The New York Times

item.title: Threat of Explosion From Toxic Chemical Tank Lessened, Officials Say
item.description: The risk of a large explosion has been averted, officials say. But a smaller blast remains possible, and 16,000 people remain displaced.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault, Heather Knight, Shawn Hubler and Christina Morales

media:content.media:description: An aerial view of water being sprayed onto a tank at GKN Aerospace in Garden Grove, Calif., on Saturday.

item.title: Trump busca hacer listas de ciudadanos en EE. UU: lo que hay que saber
item.description: El presidente Trump intenta crear listas para determinar quién puede votar, aunque su gobierno reconoce que no serían fiables.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella

media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump firmó en marzo una orden ejecutiva que instaba a los estados a utilizar bases de datos federales para identificar a los no ciudadanos.

item.title: En Cuba cocinan con carbón y leña para sobrevivir
item.description: La vida en Santiago de Cuba y otras partes de la isla ha empeorado desde que EE. UU. inició su campaña de presión contra el gobierno comunista del país.
item.dc:creator: Ed Augustin and Lisette Poole González

item.title: Clarence B. Jones, Who Helped Shape ‘I Have a Dream’ Speech, Dies at 95
item.description: A confidant and lawyer for Martin Luther King, he was an unseen hand behind major civil rights events, including the 1963 March on Washington.
item.dc:creator: Robert D. McFadden

media:content.media:credit: Marissa Leshnov for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Clarence B. Jones in 2023. A brilliant organizer, he planned protest campaigns, raised funds and coordinated legal strategies during the civil rights movement of the 1960s.

item.title: Cambodia’s Leader Pardons Top Opposition Politician Kem Sokha
item.description: The decision to release Kem Sokha is being seen as a likely attempt to recalibrate frayed ties with Western countries.
item.dc:creator: Sun Narin and Sui-Lee Wee
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Tang Chhin Sothy/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Kem Sokha, the leading opposition leader in Cambodia, arriving for the verdict in his trial in Phnom Penh in 2023.

item.title: How Lizzo Is Navigating Public Backlash As a Pop Star
item.description: Lizzo spoke to “Popcast” about making positive music amid a couple of turbulent years in the public eye.

item.title: Absent Congressman Calls Allies, but Stops Short of Public Appearance
item.description: Republican officials said they have talked to Tom Kean Jr., who has been absent from Congress for weeks. His office said Mr. Kean will eventually explain his absence.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Although Republican officials have now reported hearing his voice, Tom Kean Jr.’s whereabouts for now remain a mystery.

item.title: As A.I. Fever Rises in Silicon Valley, Pope Leo Has a Few Words
item.description: The American pope wants to take artificial intelligence down a notch. Is he challenging the tech companies, or will tech take over the papacy?
item.dc:creator: David Streitfeld

item.title: Pope Leo Warns of A.I. Risks in His First Papal Encyclical
item.description: Pope Leo XIV presented his first encyclical, “Magnifica Humanitas,” in which he outlined his desire to protect human dignity and agency in an age when technology threatens to replace people in many professional and social roles.
item.dc:creator: Axel Boada

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV at the presentation of his first encyclical letter, “Magnifica Humanitas,” at the Vatican on Monday. In it, he outlined his desire to protect human dignity and agency in an age of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Roasted Rhubarb, Harissa Shrimp and the Only Ice Cream Recipe You’ll Ever Need
item.description: Delicious, simple recipes to close out the holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Clark

media:content.media:credit: Armando Rafael for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Cyd Raftus McDowell.
media:content.media:description: Melissa Clark’s roasted rhubarb.

item.title: Ryan Speedo Green in ‘Die Walküre’: Preview of the Met Opera
item.description: Ryan Speedo Green has been previewing his take on Wotan, ruler of the gods, with Gustavo Dudamel and the Los Angeles Philharmonic.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Barone

media:content.media:description: Ryan Speedo Green, front, as Wotan in “Die Walküre,” near Christine Goerke as Brünnhilde.

item.title: How Trump’s Cabinet Speaks to Him: Praise, Accolades and Lots of Criticizing Opponents
item.description: The New York Times reviewed over a dozen hours of cabinet meeting footage to analyze how President Trump’s administration speaks to him. Many pack their speaking time with praise and flattery.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Cai

item.title: Trump’s Pressure Had Little Effect on Iran’s Terms for a Peace Deal
item.description: President Trump had sought to force Tehran to accept American demands for a peace deal with a mixture of threats and military operations.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: For weeks, the Trump administration pursued a diplomatic agreement rather than returning to war in Iran.

item.title: Even After a Strait of Hormuz Deal, Moving 1,500 Ships Won’t Be Easy
item.description: An agreement to reopen the waterway would be followed by a complicated process of navigating a backlog of vessels stranded for nearly three months.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Companies will need to know how their ships will be prioritized if passage through the Strait of Hormuz is restored.

item.title: UK Heat Wave Could Break May Temperature Record
item.description: Forecasters warned that temperatures could climb to the highest level ever recorded in the month of May, reaching 35 degrees Celsius (95 degrees Fahrenheit) in some areas of England.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar

media:content.media:description: Beachgoers in Brighton, England, on Monday. The country is facing its first heat wave of the season.
